2.5. Dataset Statistics & Class Distribution 
To address Class Imbalance, we analyzed the distribution of the unified dataset. 
 
Fig 2.5.1: Class Distribution 
 
The person category had the majority samples as we had to focus on identifying is there is 
person present ahead.  
2.6. Optimized Navigation-Centric Variation (DSAI-Unified-3Cls) 
While the primary DSAI-Unified-Dataset features 11 distinct classes for detailed

scene understanding, we developed an optimized "Lite" version specifically 
designed to prioritize navigational safety and real-time inference speed. 
2.6.1. Class Collapse & Mapping Logic 
In this variation, we transitioned from a detailed classification task to a Functional 
Hazard Model. We collapsed the original 11 classes into 3 high-priority categories 
based on their role in the navigation pipeline: 
Original Classes Unified 
Navigation Class Functional Rationale

Navigation Class Functional Rationale 
Person Person Dynamic hazard requiring high-priority avoidance 
and specialized social-distancing logic. 
Door Door Critical navigational waypoint for room-to-room 
transitions. 
Chair, Sofa, Table, 
Bed, Lamp, Plant, 
etc. 
Obstacle 
General stationary hazards. For the purpose of 
pathfinding, the specific type of furniture is 
secondary to its physical occupancy of space.
